task: Review the feat/mail branch changes vs main. Do a security review, a code quality review, and simplification review. Fix any issues you find directly. Then report what you found and fixed.

# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup¥n¥nReview all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.¥n¥n## Phase 1: Identify Changes¥n¥nRun `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.¥n¥n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel¥n¥nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.¥n¥n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review¥n¥nFor each change:¥n¥n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase ¥u2014 common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.¥n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.¥n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** ¥u2014 hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.¥n¥n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review¥n¥nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:¥n¥n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls¥n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones¥n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction¥n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries¥n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase¥n¥n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review¥n¥nReview the same changes for efficiency:¥n¥n1. **Unnece

Base directory for this skill: /home/openclaw/code/tuicraft/.claude/skills/typescript-style¥n¥n# TypeScript Style¥n¥nPrettier with defaults handles formatting.¥nThis covers structure and taste.¥n¥n## Types¥n¥n- `type` only. No `interface`, no `enum`.¥n- `as const` objects for opcode tables and constant maps.¥n- Unions for enumerations: `type Status = ¥

`¥n- Never `any`. Reserve `unknown` for true boundaries. If you know the type, use¥n  it.¥n- Discriminated unions over loose fields. `UserEntry | AgentEntry` not `{ data:¥nunknown }`.¥n- Compose types with `&` for extending, but prefer nested fields over flat¥n  intersections when callers pass a shared object. Spread objects bypass¥n  TypeScript's excess property checks ¥u2014 nesting preserves them.¥n- When 3+ functions share the same args, extract a context type and pass it as a¥n  nested field rather than repeating individual args at every call site.¥n- Extract types to keep function signatures to one line.¥n- Annotate params and return types. Skip the annotation when the type is inferred¥n  from a default value (`now = new Date()` not `now: Date = new Date()`).¥n- Absolute imports via baseUrl. No `.ts` extensions.¥n¥n## Functions¥n¥n- `function` for named/exported. Arrows for callbacks only.¥n- 3-10 lines ideal. 30 lines hard max.¥n- Never break an argument list across multiple lines. If Prettier would wrap the¥n  args, extract a type and take a single arg object instead.¥n- Destructure in the signature when it fits one line. Move to the body when it¥n  gets wide.¥n- Guards grouped at the top. Symmetry in sequential ifs.¥n- Build symmetrical helper pairs so call sites read like prose.¥n- Group related variables together at the top of the body.¥n- Functions should call their own dependencies ¥u2014 don't make callers pass in what¥n  the function can get itself.¥n- Pull nested config objects into named variables above the call that uses them.¥n- Prefer template literals over `[...].join(¥
